page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i3.13xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. This instance features 52 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core configuration, providing 52 vCPUs. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.38 GB of RAM per core. The KVM-virtualized instance includes 11519 GB of bundled local SSD storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

The hardware profile of the ecs.i3.13xlarge is characterized by its high-capacity local SSD storage and dedicated CPU allocation. Because the CPU threads-per-core ratio is 1.0, each vCPU maps to a dedicated physical core without hyperthreading, which reduces resource contention and ensures predictable compute performance. However, the lack of hyperthreading means the absolute parallel thread count is limited to 52. The massive 11.5 TB local SSD storage makes this instance highly resource-dense, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-capacity, low-latency local storage without the overhead of network-attached storage. The instance does not include GPU acceleration.

Given its substantial local SSD capacity and balanced memory-to-CPU ratio, the ecs.i3.13xlarge is optimized for storage-intensive and database workloads. It is highly suitable for hosting large relational databases, NoSQL databases, distributed file systems, and data warehousing applications. The dedicated CPU cores and 16 Gbps network bandwidth also support transactional processing and high-throughput data analytics pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i3.13xlarge is an x86_64 server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 52 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 52 vCPUs and eliminating hyperthreading resource contention. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, offering 7.38 GB of memory per core, and includes 11519 GB of bundled local SSD storage alongside a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this configuration provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for storage-heavy deployments. The absence of GPU acceleration and hyperthreading are key architectural tradeoffs. This instance is optimized for large-scale databases, distributed file systems, and high-throughput transactional workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 7.38 GB of RAM per core.
